titleStmt.title: Letter from May Fay to James Finn, 22 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: May Fay

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A short card from May Fay (1897-1976) to her fiance James (1877-1922) stating that she's looking forward to a visit.James Finn was a civil servant, working in the National Health Service Commission on Mount Street. May Fay became engaged to James Finn in January 1916. Despite the twenty year age gap their letters show a very personal and private insight into courting in early 20th century Ireland. While neither James nor May were politically active their letters provide a very unique insight into the affairs of Easter Week and its aftermath. James died in 1922 as a result of the flu epidemic, May was pregnant with their fourth child at the time. She held on to his letters as her reminder of him. May never remarried and focused her attention instead on her businesses in order to provide for their children

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
M.Gar James Dearest, I was very glad to get
your letter this morn it cheered
me as I was feeling lonely not
feeling so well & then Mother
living away fancy she never
wrote us since she left but asked
in a letter to Mrs. S. did we get
her card. She is staying till
Monday but when that day comes shell
will stay until another I hope when
she does come I will feel fit to start
on that so much wished for visit
Hope you will excuse this short
note in answer to your nice letter Your ever loving May I met Mrs.PriceMorgan today she wanted to know from Mrs. Sâ
was I the one
was engaged
to her friend
It was a
start to see
it had legs
so soon
good news
you know
J.E.Finn Esq. 39 Belgrave Sq. Rathmines Dublin
